tradeReviews.reason: Had a good entry for puts on Qs but panicked on the bounce, got out for a loss. Position kept dropping after exit.
tradeReviews.lesson: Don't panic on bounces during a trending move; let the trade play out if the thesis is intact
tradeReviews.author: WillDaBeast

tradeReviews.reason: Took the immediate retest into market open and lost instead of waiting for the 84% retrace which would have been a 9R winner
tradeReviews.lesson: Wait for proper retrace (84% rule) rather than jumping in at market open
tradeReviews.author: Sam Nuho

tradeReviews.reason: Stayed in the trade using order blocks as resistance levels, locked in $447 profit
tradeReviews.lesson: Use order blocks as resistance to stay in trades longer
tradeReviews.author: Addy

tradeReviews.reason: Trade was up $230+ and about to hit LOD, missed by 0.04%, then totally reversed to a $120 loss. Followed rules but still lost.
tradeReviews.lesson: Use limit orders to secure TP at key levels and avoid missing by fractions
tradeReviews.author: DWC2016

tradeReviews.reason: Nice 5-minute break and retest at 9:44am, bought 4 long calls at $2.39, sold at $3.90 for $604 profit in 18 minutes
tradeReviews.lesson: Need to learn to stay in trades longer for bigger moves
tradeReviews.author: Addy

tradeReviews.reason: Was up $115 at one point but held too long/got greedy, sold during consolidation for a small loss right before it spiked to a new high of day
tradeReviews.lesson: Don't get greedy — take profits when available or at least move stops to breakeven
tradeReviews.author: Ryan

tradeReviews.reason: Took a loss on first entry and then on the 84% rule re-entry, but looking back should not have stopped out because the 5-min chart looked good
tradeReviews.lesson: Trust the higher timeframe structure even during drawdown; don't let intraday noise shake you out
tradeReviews.author: habob

tradeReviews.reason: First trade had a loss of about $180 per contract due to the second down closed candle pushing too far, ended with $60 total loss on two trades
tradeReviews.lesson: Consider candle close stop loss to avoid being stopped by wick extensions
tradeReviews.author: unnas

tradeReviews.reason: Bought SPX puts but did not buy them at a clean entry point, had to manage risk and exit suboptimally
tradeReviews.lesson: Wait for a clean entry point — don't enter just because you want to be in the trade
tradeReviews.author: rocketup

tradeReviews.reason: Solid trade overall, but should have targeted Low Of Day for maximum profit
tradeReviews.lesson: Aim for LOD on MNQ shorts when trend is strong
tradeReviews.author: Luke

tradeReviews.reason: Solid trade though it never technically touched the PML (came very close). Per strict rules it needs to wick into or touch the level.
tradeReviews.lesson: Be strict about PML touches — close isn't good enough for rule-based entries
tradeReviews.author: Luke

tradeReviews.reason: Took the 1:30pm reversal, waited for break and retest of premarket lows at 19,216.25, made a solid profit
tradeReviews.lesson: N/A
tradeReviews.author: Mez

tradeReviews.reason: Took TSLA right after break of day high with no retest and it flew to the upside. Usually waits for retest but the strong displacement worked.
tradeReviews.lesson: Strong displacement can justify entries without a retest
tradeReviews.author: Kash

tradeReviews.reason: Original entry wasn't great — it retraced down $18 — but bought more to average down and eventually had a huge swing win. Bad entry saved by good position management.
tradeReviews.lesson: Scaling into a position (averaging down) can save a bad entry if the thesis is intact
tradeReviews.author: GoodVibez

tradeReviews.reason: Had to cut AMD early because couldn't monitor the trade after 8 AM, lost about $25 of profit and took a hit on the R multiple
tradeReviews.lesson: Use limit orders or avoid entering trades you can't monitor to completion
tradeReviews.author: Viper

tradeReviews.reason: Took HOOD calls using the 1 candle rule, executed solidly
tradeReviews.lesson: N/A
tradeReviews.author: John H

tradeReviews.reason: Got stopped out on AAPL 205 calls runners at $9, now they are $20+. Stopped out too early on runners in a winner.
tradeReviews.lesson: Consider wider stops on runners or trailing stops instead of fixed levels in strong trends
tradeReviews.author: NT

tradeReviews.reason: Good moves on first two NVDA trades, made $44
tradeReviews.lesson: N/A
tradeReviews.author: Patel_Trades

tradeReviews.reason: Took a stupid AAPL move in between NVDA trades — it was an emotional trade that cost $53
tradeReviews.lesson: Don't take emotional trades between good setups; stick to the plan
tradeReviews.author: Patel_Trades

tradeReviews.reason: Caught PLTR bouncing off 184 for a good trade
tradeReviews.lesson: N/A
tradeReviews.author: Alkanall

tradeReviews.reason: Re-entered AMD but was tired and thought it might go sideways, exited early. A few minutes later it ran. Exited due to fatigue.
tradeReviews.lesson: Don't let fatigue cause premature exits — use a game plan not feelings
tradeReviews.author: Alkanall

tradeReviews.reason: Beautiful NVDA play at the summit — worked out perfectly in front of thousands. Demonstrates the system works if you follow the rules.
tradeReviews.lesson: The system works if you follow the rules consistently
tradeReviews.author: Davis

tradeReviews.reason: Should have held the NVDA swing longer — sold yesterday, it continued today. Still happy with the profit though.
tradeReviews.lesson: Hold swing trades longer when the trend is intact
tradeReviews.author: JMURR

tradeReviews.reason: Took a big loss on NVDA and didn't have the courage to re-enter on the 84% rule, but the re-entry would have worked out
tradeReviews.lesson: Have the courage to re-enter on the 84% rule if the setup is still valid — don't let a loss shake your confidence
tradeReviews.author: LifeWithMikey

tradeReviews.reason: Late entries on TSLA led to losing more % than wanted, then cut the TSLL trade too early due to frustration from the NVDA loss
tradeReviews.lesson: Late entries compound losses; sit out rather than chase
tradeReviews.author: LifeWithMikey

tradeReviews.reason: Went long over 427 targeting PDH but didn't get follow through. Setup was correct (avoided no-trade zone) but the move didn't materialize.
tradeReviews.lesson: Trust your levels and process regardless of outcome — the trade was valid
tradeReviews.author: Markellwhite16

tradeReviews.reason: Super solid trade catching the PDL break and retest
tradeReviews.lesson: N/A
tradeReviews.author: Markellwhite16

tradeReviews.reason: Break and retest candles were too tiny and the ATH nearby has sell-side liquidity — not a good setup despite being a clean level
tradeReviews.lesson: Need strong candle confirmation on break and retest, especially near ATH levels with sell-side liquidity overhead
tradeReviews.author: FraggDieb

tradeReviews.reason: 3rd trade on AMD was good, but didn't hold through the $2 move up and down — exited early in the volatility
tradeReviews.lesson: Hold through intraday noise if the thesis is intact
tradeReviews.author: Limitless

tradeReviews.reason: FOMO after SPY moved up, then made a series of emotional/bad trades. Down $400.
tradeReviews.lesson: Don't FOMO — emotional trading after missing a move leads to compounding losses
tradeReviews.author: Patel_Trades

tradeReviews.reason: PMHR (Pre-Market High Retest) was a perfect entry setup
tradeReviews.lesson: N/A
tradeReviews.author: Jonathan

tradeReviews.reason: $77 loss on TSLA with one contract, but positive because respected the stop loss and followed the plan. Good process despite losing trade.
tradeReviews.lesson: Following your plan and respecting stop losses matters more than individual trade outcomes
tradeReviews.author: jwolfe29

tradeReviews.reason: Sold AMD on the HOD (high of day) pop, good exit execution
tradeReviews.lesson: N/A
tradeReviews.author: jwolfe29

tradeReviews.reason: Loss on AMD was a combination of late entry and an execution mistake on the first take-profit target — missed it by a couple of seconds
tradeReviews.lesson: Improve execution speed and consider using limit orders for TP targets
tradeReviews.author: Hugo Comte

tradeReviews.reason: Lost on AAPL but the trade was still valid — had seen great moves developing after open consolidation in past days, just didn't work this time
tradeReviews.lesson: Valid setups can still lose; that's probability, not a flaw in the system
tradeReviews.author: Hugo Comte

tradeReviews.reason: Accidentally entered short when trying to go long (clicked wrong side on broker), cut immediately but lost $100 on the mistake
tradeReviews.lesson: Double-check order direction before submitting and cut accidental entries immediately
tradeReviews.author: Limitless

tradeReviews.reason: Had a great entry on TSLA with 3 contracts, plan was to take off 2 on the pop but accidentally hit buy instead of sell, then panic sold as soon as green. Only made $67.
tradeReviews.lesson: Slow down and verify order buttons before clicking; don't panic sell after a mistake
tradeReviews.author: jwolfe29

tradeReviews.reason: Took profit too early on a TSLA short because feared a reversal, but should have waited for market structure to break. Missed what would have been the one good trade for the month.
tradeReviews.lesson: Let winners run until market structure breaks — don't exit on fear of reversal
tradeReviews.author: Jai
